subject: auth command
old: auth command did not exist or had different behavior
new: auth command now enables certificate-based authentication to obtain Kerberos tickets, NT hashes, or establish LDAP connections
truth: The 'auth' subcommand is now available for certificate-based authentication to obtain Kerberos tickets, NT hashes, or establish LDAP connections.
quote: Authenticate to Active Directory services using certificates. This command enables certificate-based authentication to obtain Kerberos tickets, NT hashes, or establish LDAP connections.

from_fact: auth command did not exist or had different behavior
auth command now enables certificate-based authentication to obtain kerberos tickets, nt hashes, or establish ldap connections

subject: export output options
old: In version 4.x, -export was used with -out to specify output filename for PFX/P12
new: In version 5, -export is a boolean flag (default format) and -out is a separate option for output filename
truth: Stop using -export with a filename argument; -export is now a boolean flag and -out must be used separately to specify the output filename.
quote:
-export               Export to PFX/P12 file (default format)
  -out outfile          Output filename for the exported certificate/key

from_fact: in version 4.x, -export was used with -out to specify output filename for pfx/p12
in version 5, -export is a boolean flag (default format) and -out is a separate option for output filename

subject: find command output options
old: In version 4.x, -output was used to specify a filename for output
new: In version 5, -output is a filename prefix, not a full filename
truth: Stop using -output as a full filename; -output now specifies a filename prefix and the tool appends extensions automatically.
quote: -output prefix        Filename prefix for writing results to

from_fact: in version 4.x, -output was used to specify a filename for output
in version 5, -output is a filename prefix, not a full filename

subject: find command output options
old: In version 4.x, -text was the default output format
new: In version 5, -text is an explicit flag to output as formatted text file
truth: Stop relying on -text being the default; you must now explicitly pass -text to get text file output, or use -stdout for console output.
quote:
-text                 Output result as formatted text file
  -stdout               Output result as text directly to console

from_fact: in version 4.x, -text was the default output format
in version 5, -text is an explicit flag to output as formatted text file

subject: default LDAP scheme
truth: The default LDAP connection scheme changed from ldap to ldaps, so existing scripts relying on unencrypted LDAP will now fail unless -ldap-scheme ldap is explicitly passed.
quote: LDAP connection scheme to use (default: ldaps)

subject: default LDAP port
truth: The default LDAP port changed from 389 to 636 because the default scheme is now ldaps, so connections to port 389 will fail unless -ldap-port 389 and -ldap-scheme ldap are explicitly set.
quote: Port for LDAP communication (default: 636 for ldaps, 389 for ldap)

subject: -debug flag position
truth: The -debug flag is now global and must be placed before the subcommand, not after it.
quote: The -debug flag is now GLOBAL and must be placed BEFORE the subcommand, not after it.

subject: BloodHound integration
truth: BloodHound integration has been removed; functionality is now native to BloodHound.
quote: BloodHound integration retired (functionality now native to BloodHound).

subject: Windows-specific features (SSPI, PTT ticket injection)
truth: Windows-specific features including SSPI and PTT ticket injection have been removed; use tools like Rubeus instead.
quote: Windows-specific features removed (SSPI, PTT ticket injection — replaced by tools like Rubeus).

subject: Output file overwrite behavior
truth: Certipy no longer silently overwrites output files; users receive prompts if output files exist, with auto-generated random suffixes as fallback.
quote: No more silent file overwrites — users receive prompts if output files exist, with auto-generated random suffixes as fallback.
